515	\section{The dimension-shift step}
516	
517	The induction driving the comparison theorem rests on a single short-exact-sequence
518	calculation: cutting a resolution into short exact sequences and reading off the
519	long exact sequence of right-derived functors. The latter is not available off the
520	shelf at the level of the derived-functor objects \((R^k G)\); we build it from the
521	complex-level homology long exact sequence
522	(Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) via the horseshoe lift
523	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}). We first record the shift at the
524	\emph{resolution} level — where the horseshoe lift directly supplies the
525	degreewise-split short exact sequence of injective resolutions — and then specialize
526	to objects.
527	
528	\begin{lemma}[Resolution-level dimension shift]
529	  \label{lem:right_derived_shift_split_resolution}
530	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfSplitResolutionSES, CategoryTheory.shortExact_of_degreewise_splitting, CategoryTheory.shortExact_map_mapHomologicalComplex_of_degreewise_splitting, CategoryTheory.Functor.isZero_homology_mapHomologicalComplex_of_isRightAcyclic}
531	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
532	    lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
533	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be additive and let
534	  \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\) be a short exact sequence with \(J\) right-\(G\)-acyclic,
535	  lifted to a \emph{degreewise-split} short exact sequence of injective resolutions
536	  \(0 \to I_A \to I_J \to I_Z \to 0\) (chain maps \(\varphi, \psi\) with
537	  \(\psi \circ \varphi = 0\) and a splitting of \(0 \to I_A^n \to I_J^n \to I_Z^n \to 0\)
538	  in each degree \(n\)). Then for every \(k \geq 0\) the connecting map of the homology
539	  long exact sequence of \(G(I_\bullet)\) is an isomorphism
540	  \[
541	    (R^{k+1} G)(Z) \;\cong\; (R^{k+2} G)(A).
542	  \]
543	\end{lemma}
544	
545	\begin{proof}
546	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_injective_resolution,
547	    lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}
548	  Applying \(G\) degreewise to the degreewise-split sequence preserves each split
549	  degree, yielding a short exact sequence of cochain complexes
550	  \(0 \to G(I_A) \to G(I_J) \to G(I_Z) \to 0\) in \(\mathcal{B}\). Its homology long
551	  exact sequence (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) has connecting maps
552	  \(\delta^m : H^m(G(I_Z)) \to H^{m+1}(G(I_A))\); transport these across the
553	  injective-resolution computation \(H^m(G(I_-)) \cong (R^m G)(-)\)
554	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution}). For \(m = k+1 \geq 1\) the two
555	  homology objects flanking \(\delta^m\) in the long exact sequence both sit on the
556	  middle complex \(G(I_J)\), namely \(H^{k+1}(G(I_J))\) and \(H^{k+2}(G(I_J))\); both
557	  vanish because \(J\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic (Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}).
558	  With both neighbours zero, \(\delta^{k+1}\) is an isomorphism, giving
559	  \((R^{k+1} G)(Z) \cong (R^{k+2} G)(A)\).
560	\end{proof}

562	\begin{lemma}\leanok
563	[Dimension shift across an acyclic short exact sequence]
564	  \label{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}
565	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.rightDerivedShiftIsoOfAcyclic}
566	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
567	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
568	    lem:right_derived_shift_split_resolution}
569	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Lemma
570	  % \texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses} (Tag 015D)
571	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5619--5654).
572	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $F : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ be a left exact functor
573	  % between abelian categories and assume
574	  % $RF : D^{+}(\mathcal{A}) \to D^{+}(\mathcal{B})$ is everywhere
575	  % defined. Let $0 \to A \to B \to C \to 0$ be a short exact sequence
576	  % of $\mathcal{A}$.
577	  % \begin{enumerate}
578	  % \item If $A$ and $C$ are right acyclic for $F$ then so is $B$.
579	  % \item If $A$ and $B$ are right acyclic for $F$ then so is $C$.
580	  % \item If $B$ and $C$ are right acyclic for $F$ and $F(B) \to F(C)$ is

582	  % \end{enumerate}
583	  % In each of the three cases
584	  % $$
585	  % 0 \to F(A) \to F(B) \to F(C) \to 0
586	  % $$
587	  % is a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{B}$."
588	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 015D
589	  (\texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses}).}
590	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be an additive left-exact functor, and let
591	  \[
592	    0 \longrightarrow A \longrightarrow J \longrightarrow Z \longrightarrow 0
593	  \]
594	  be a short exact sequence in \(\mathcal{A}\) with the middle term \(J\)
595	  right-\(G\)-acyclic. Then the connecting maps of the long exact sequence of
596	  right-derived functors furnish isomorphisms
597	  \[
598	    (R^{k} G)(Z) \;\xrightarrow{\ \cong\ }\; (R^{k+1} G)(A)
599	    \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
600	  \]
601	  (The companion lowest-degree statement
602	  \((R^{1} G)(A) \cong \operatorname{coker}(G(J) \to G(Z))\) is isolated as the
603	  sibling Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}.)
604	\end{lemma}
605	
606	% The Stacks proof of Tag 015D derives the long exact sequence from the
607	% delta-functor structure of $RF$ on the derived category $D^{+}(\mathcal{A})$.
608	% The proof below constructs the same sequence at the level of right-derived-functor
609	% objects from the complex-level homology long exact sequence
610	% (Lemma \ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) after a horseshoe lift
611	% (Lemma \ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}); accordingly no verbatim proof
612	% quote from Tag 015D is attached.
613	\begin{proof}
614	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
615	    lem:right_derived_injective_resolution, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
616	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses, lem:right_derived_shift_split_resolution}
617	    \leanok
618	  We first construct the long exact sequence of right-derived functors associated
619	  to \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\). In the classical treatment this sequence is
620	  supplied by the \(\delta\)-functor structure of \(\{R^k G\}\) on the derived
621	  category; here it is built directly from the complex-level homology long exact
622	  sequence via a horseshoe lift.
623	
624	  \emph{Building the long exact sequence.} By the horseshoe lift
625	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}) choose injective resolutions
626	  \(I_A, I_J, I_Z\) of \(A, J, Z\) fitting into a short exact sequence of cochain
627	  complexes
628	  \[
629	    0 \longrightarrow I_A \longrightarrow I_J \longrightarrow I_Z \longrightarrow 0
630	  \]
631	  that is degreewise split. Apply \(G\) degreewise. Because each degree
632	  \(0 \to I_A^n \to I_J^n \to I_Z^n \to 0\) is a \emph{split} short exact sequence
633	  (so \(I_J^n \cong I_A^n \oplus I_Z^n\) compatibly with the maps), the additive
634	  functor \(G\) carries it to a split short exact sequence
635	  \(0 \to G(I_A^n) \to G(I_J^n) \to G(I_Z^n) \to 0\) --- this is where degreewise
636	  splitness is essential, since \(G\) is not assumed exact and would not in general
637	  preserve a non-split short exact sequence. Hence
638	  \[
639	    0 \longrightarrow G(I_A) \longrightarrow G(I_J) \longrightarrow G(I_Z)
640	      \longrightarrow 0
641	  \]
642	  is a short exact sequence of cochain complexes in \(\mathcal{B}\). The
643	  complex-level homology long exact sequence
644	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}) applied to it reads
645	  \[
646	    \cdots \to H^k\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to H^k\bigl(G(I_Z)\bigr)
647	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_A)\bigr)
648	      \to H^{k+1}\bigl(G(I_J)\bigr) \to \cdots .
649	  \]
650	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution} the homology of each applied
651	  resolution computes the right-derived functor:
652	  \(H^k(G(I_A)) \cong (R^k G)(A)\), \(H^k(G(I_J)) \cong (R^k G)(J)\) and
653	  \(H^k(G(I_Z)) \cong (R^k G)(Z)\), naturally in the maps of the sequence. Transporting
654	  the homology long exact sequence across these isomorphisms gives the long exact
655	  sequence of right-derived functors
656	  \[
657	    \cdots \to (R^k G)(J) \to (R^k G)(Z)
658	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to (R^{k+1} G)(J) \to \cdots,
659	  \]
660	  and, using \(R^0 G \cong G\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}) at the
661	  bottom, its low-degree initial segment
662	  \[
663	    0 \to G(A) \to G(J) \to G(Z)
664	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A) \to (R^1 G)(J) \to \cdots .
665	  \]
666	
667	  \emph{Killing the acyclic terms.} Because \(J\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic
668	  (Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}), every term \((R^k G)(J)\) with \(k \geq 1\)
669	  vanishes. For \(k \geq 1\) the segment around \((R^k G)(J)\),
670	  \[
671	    \underbrace{(R^{k} G)(J)}_{=0} \to (R^{k} G)(Z)
672	      \xrightarrow{\ \delta^k\ } (R^{k+1} G)(A) \to \underbrace{(R^{k+1} G)(J)}_{=0},
673	  \]
674	  has both outer terms zero, so the connecting map
675	  \(\delta^k : (R^{k} G)(Z) \to (R^{k+1} G)(A)\) is an isomorphism, which is the
676	  asserted dimension shift. (The lowest-degree segment of the same long exact
677	  sequence yields the cokernel description of \((R^1 G)(A)\); that reading is
678	  recorded separately in Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}.)
679	\end{proof}

683	  \label{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}

698	  % (Lemma \ref{lemma-left-exact-higher-derived})
699	  % we obtain an exact cohomology sequence

703	  (\texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses}).}
704	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be an additive left-exact functor, and let
705	  \[

709	  right-\(G\)-acyclic. Then there is an isomorphism in \(\mathcal{B}\)

717	\begin{proof}
718	  \uses{def:right_acyclic, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self,
719	    lem:right_derived_injective_resolution, lem:homology_long_exact_sequence,
720	    lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}

722	  This reads off the lowest-degree segment of the very long exact sequence of
723	  right-derived functors built, from the same short exact sequence
724	  \(0 \to A \to J \to Z \to 0\), in the proof of
725	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift}; here we use only its initial terms, so we
726	  recall the construction briefly and then specialize. By the horseshoe lift
727	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_resolution_of_ses}) choose injective resolutions
728	  \(I_A, I_J, I_Z\) of \(A, J, Z\) fitting into a degreewise-split short exact
729	  sequence of cochain complexes \(0 \to I_A \to I_J \to I_Z \to 0\). Applying the
730	  additive functor \(G\) degreewise preserves each split degree, giving a short exact
731	  sequence of complexes \(0 \to G(I_A) \to G(I_J) \to G(I_Z) \to 0\) in
732	  \(\mathcal{B}\); its complex-level homology long exact sequence
733	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:homology_long_exact_sequence}), transported across the
734	  injective-resolution computation \(H^k(G(I_\bullet)) \cong (R^k G)(-)\)
735	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_injective_resolution}) and using
736	  \(R^0 G \cong G\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}), has low-degree
737	  initial segment

739	    G(J) \to G(Z) \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A) \to (R^1 G)(J).
740	  \]

742	  end \((R^1 G)(J)\) vanishes. Hence the segment
743	  \(G(J) \to G(Z) \xrightarrow{\ \delta^0\ } (R^1 G)(A) \to 0\) is exact with vanishing

750	\section{Cosyzygies of an acyclic resolution}
751	
752	The comparison theorem decomposes an acyclic resolution into the short exact
753	sequences cut out by its cosyzygies, and reads the cohomology of the applied complex
754	off those sequences using left-exactness of \(G\). We isolate the two ingredients
755	here.
756	
757	\begin{lemma}[Cosyzygy short exact sequences of an acyclic resolution]
758	  \label{lem:cosyzygy_ses}
759	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.cosyzygyShortExact, CategoryTheory.Functor.cosyzygyShortComplex, CategoryTheory.cosyzygyKernelFork, CategoryTheory.cosyzygy_iCycles_comp_toCycles, CategoryTheory.epi_toCycles_of_exactAt}
760	  \uses{def:right_acyclic}
761	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Proposition
762	  % \texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics} (Tag 05TA)
763	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5785--5838).
764	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Say $I^n = 0$ for $n < n_0$. Setting $J^n = \Im(d^n)$ we break
765	  % $I^\bullet$ into short exact sequences
766	  % $0 \to J^n \to I^{n + 1} \to J^{n + 1} \to 0$
767	  % for $n \geq n_0$."
768	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 05TA
769	  (\texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics}).}
770	  Let \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) be additive and let
771	  \[
772	    0 \longrightarrow A
773	      \longrightarrow J^0 \longrightarrow J^1 \longrightarrow J^2
774	      \longrightarrow \cdots
775	  \]
776	  be an exact resolution of \(A\) with every \(J^n\) right-\(G\)-acyclic. Define the
777	  cosyzygies \(Z^0 := A\) and, for \(m \geq 1\),
778	  \[
779	    Z^m \;:=\; \ker\bigl(J^m \to J^{m+1}\bigr)
780	      \;=\; \operatorname{im}\bigl(J^{m-1} \to J^m\bigr).
781	  \]
782	  Then for every \(m \geq 0\) the sequence
783	  \[
784	    0 \longrightarrow Z^m \longrightarrow J^m \longrightarrow Z^{m+1}
785	      \longrightarrow 0 \tag{$S_m$}
786	  \]
787	  is short exact, with the inclusion \(Z^m \hookrightarrow J^m\) the canonical one,
788	  the surjection \(J^m \twoheadrightarrow Z^{m+1}\) the corestriction of
789	  \(J^m \to J^{m+1}\) onto its image, and the middle term \(J^m\) right-\(G\)-acyclic.
790	  In particular \(Z^1 = \operatorname{im}(J^0 \to J^1) = \operatorname{coker}(A \to J^0)\).
791	\end{lemma}
792	
793	\begin{proof}
794	  \uses{def:right_acyclic}
795	  Fix \(m \geq 0\). Mono of \(Z^m \hookrightarrow J^m\) is immediate: \(Z^m\) is by
796	  definition a subobject of \(J^m\) (for \(m = 0\) via the injection \(A \hookrightarrow
797	  J^0\) of the resolution, for \(m \geq 1\) as the kernel \(\ker(J^m \to J^{m+1})\)).
798	  The map \(J^m \to Z^{m+1}\) is the corestriction of the differential
799	  \(d^m : J^m \to J^{m+1}\) onto its image \(Z^{m+1} = \operatorname{im}(d^m)\), hence
800	  an epimorphism. Exactness in the middle is exactness of the resolution at \(J^m\):
801	  the image of \(Z^m \hookrightarrow J^m\) equals \(\ker(d^m)\) --- for \(m \geq 1\)
802	  by the definition \(Z^m = \ker(J^m \to J^{m+1})\), and for \(m = 0\) because
803	  exactness of \(0 \to A \to J^0 \to J^1\) makes \(A \hookrightarrow J^0\) identify
804	  \(A\) with \(\ker(d^0)\) --- while the kernel of the epimorphism
805	  \(J^m \twoheadrightarrow Z^{m+1}\) is exactly \(\ker(d^m)\). Thus
806	  \(\operatorname{im}(Z^m \to J^m) = \ker(J^m \to Z^{m+1})\), so \((S_m)\) is short
807	  exact. The middle term \(J^m\) is right-\(G\)-acyclic by hypothesis. The final
808	  identity \(Z^1 = \operatorname{im}(J^0 \to J^1) = \operatorname{coker}(A \to J^0)\)
809	  is the case \(m = 0\): \(\operatorname{coker}(A \to J^0) = \operatorname{coker}(Z^0 \to J^0)
810	  \cong Z^1\) by \((S_0)\).
811	\end{proof}
812	
813	\begin{lemma}[Applied cosyzygy is the cocycle object of the applied complex]
814	  \label{lem:applied_cosyzygy_cycles}
815	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles, CategoryTheory.Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_hom_iCycles, CategoryTheory.Functor.gCosyzygyIsoCocycles_toCycles}
816	  \uses{lem:cosyzygy_ses, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}
817	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Lemma
818	  % \texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses} (Tag 015D) — the left-exact initial segment
819	  % $0 \to F(A) \to F(B) \to F(C)$ specialized to the cosyzygy sequences
820	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5619--5654).
821	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "we obtain an exact cohomology sequence
822	  % $0 \to F(A) \to F(B) \to F(C) \to R^1F(A) \to \ldots$
823	  % in the abelian category $\mathcal{B}$."
824	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 015D
825	  (\texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses}), left-exact initial segment.}
826	  With \(G\) additive left exact and the acyclic resolution and cosyzygies of
827	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cosyzygy_ses}, for every \(n \geq 0\) the morphism \(G\) applies to
828	  the inclusion \(Z^n \hookrightarrow J^n\) to give an isomorphism
829	  \[
830	    G(Z^n) \;\xrightarrow{\ \cong\ }\; \ker\bigl(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})\bigr),
831	  \]
832	  identifying \(G(Z^n)\), as a subobject of \(G(J^n)\), with the \(n\)-th cocycle
833	  object of the applied complex \(G(J^\bullet)\). For \(n = 0\) this reads
834	  \(G(A) \cong \ker(G(J^0) \to G(J^1)) = H^0\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr)\).
835	\end{lemma}
836	
837	\begin{proof}
838	  \uses{lem:cosyzygy_ses, lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}
839	  Fix \(n \geq 0\). Apply the left-exact functor \(G\) to the cosyzygy short exact
840	  sequence \((S_n)\), \(0 \to Z^n \to J^n \to Z^{n+1} \to 0\) of
841	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cosyzygy_ses}; left-exactness yields an exact sequence
842	  \[
843	    0 \longrightarrow G(Z^n) \longrightarrow G(J^n) \longrightarrow G(Z^{n+1}),
844	  \]
845	  so \(G(Z^n) \hookrightarrow G(J^n)\) is the kernel of \(G(J^n) \to G(Z^{n+1})\).
846	  Next, the differential \(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})\) of the applied complex factors as
847	  \(G(J^n) \xrightarrow{G(J^n \twoheadrightarrow Z^{n+1})} G(Z^{n+1})
848	  \xrightarrow{G(Z^{n+1} \hookrightarrow J^{n+1})} G(J^{n+1})\), because the original
849	  differential \(J^n \to J^{n+1}\) factors through its image \(Z^{n+1}\) as
850	  \(J^n \twoheadrightarrow Z^{n+1} \hookrightarrow J^{n+1}\) and \(G\) preserves
851	  composition. The second factor \(G(Z^{n+1}) \to G(J^{n+1})\) is a monomorphism (the
852	  image of the left-exact application of \(G\) to \((S_{n+1})\), whose first map
853	  \(G(Z^{n+1}) \hookrightarrow G(J^{n+1})\) is mono). A monomorphism does not change
854	  kernels under precomposition, so
855	  \(\ker(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})) = \ker(G(J^n) \to G(Z^{n+1})) = G(Z^n)\). Hence
856	  \(G(Z^n) \xrightarrow{\cong} \ker(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1}))\) as subobjects of
857	  \(G(J^n)\). For \(n = 0\), \(Z^0 = A\) gives
858	  \(G(A) \cong \ker(G(J^0) \to G(J^1)) = H^0(G(J^\bullet))\), the last equality being
859	  the definition of the zeroth cohomology of a cochain complex starting in degree
860	  \(0\) (no incoming differential). The use of \(R^0 G \cong G\)
861	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}) is what licenses reading \(G(A)\) as
862	  \((R^0 G)(A)\) when this identification feeds the comparison theorem.
863	\end{proof}

865	\begin{lemma}[Cohomology of the applied resolution as a cosyzygy cokernel]
866	  \label{lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution}
867	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.cohomologyAppliedResolutionIso, CategoryTheory.Functor.gHomologyZeroIso}

873	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Derived Categories, Proposition

876	  % (read from references/homological-acyclic-derived.tex, L5785--5838 and L5619--5654).
877	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Setting $J^n = \Im(d^n)$ we break

879	  % $0 \to J^n \to I^{n + 1} \to J^{n + 1} \to 0$
880	  % for $n \geq n_0$."
881	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 05TA
882	  (\texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics}) and Tag 015D
883	  (\texttt{lemma-F-acyclic-ses}).}
884	  With \(G\) additive left exact and the acyclic resolution and cosyzygies of
885	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cosyzygy_ses}, the cohomology of the applied complex
886	  \(G(J^\bullet) = \bigl(G(J^0) \to G(J^1) \to \cdots\bigr)\) is computed by
887	  \[
888	    H^0\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr) \;\cong\; G(A),
889	    \qquad
890	    H^n\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr) \;\cong\;
891	      \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\bigr) \quad (n \geq 1),
892	  \]
893	  where \(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\) is \(G\) applied to the surjection
894	  \(J^{n-1} \twoheadrightarrow Z^n\) of \((S_{n-1})\).
895	\end{lemma}

897	\begin{proof}

900	  Lemma~\ref{lem:applied_cosyzygy_cycles}: \(H^0(G(J^\bullet)) = \ker(G(J^0) \to G(J^1))
901	  \cong G(Z^0) = G(A)\). Fix now \(n \geq 1\). By definition of the cohomology of the
902	  cochain complex \(G(J^\bullet)\) in positive degree,
903	  \[
904	    H^n\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr)
905	      \;=\; \ker\bigl(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})\bigr) \big/
906	            \operatorname{im}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(J^n)\bigr).
907	  \]
908	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:applied_cosyzygy_cycles} the numerator is
909	  \(\ker(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})) \cong G(Z^n)\), realized as the subobject
910	  \(G(Z^n) \hookrightarrow G(J^n)\). Under this identification the differential
911	  \(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(J^n)\) lands inside \(G(Z^n)\): it factors as
912	  \(G(J^{n-1}) \xrightarrow{G(J^{n-1} \twoheadrightarrow Z^n)} G(Z^n)
913	  \hookrightarrow G(J^n)\) by the factorization of \(J^{n-1} \to J^n\) through its image
914	  \(Z^n\), so its image equals the image of \(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\) inside the
915	  subobject \(G(Z^n)\). Therefore the quotient
916	  \(\ker(G(J^n) \to G(J^{n+1})) / \operatorname{im}(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(J^n))\) is exactly
917	  \(G(Z^n) / \operatorname{im}(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)) =
918	  \operatorname{coker}(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n))\). Hence
919	  \(H^n(G(J^\bullet)) \cong \operatorname{coker}(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n))\).
920	\end{proof}

953	  % and we obtain an exact functor
954	  % $$
955	  % RF : D^{+}(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow D^{+}(\mathcal{B})
956	  % $$

958	  % $A^\bullet$ whose terms are acyclic for $RF$ computes $RF$."
959	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Derived Categories, Tag 015E (Leray's acyclicity
960	  lemma) and Tag 05TA (\texttt{proposition-enough-acyclics}).}
961	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be an abelian category with enough injectives, \(\mathcal{B}\)
962	  abelian, and \(G : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}\) an additive left-exact functor.
963	  Let
964	  \[
965	    0 \longrightarrow A
966	      \longrightarrow J^0 \longrightarrow J^1 \longrightarrow J^2
967	      \longrightarrow \cdots
968	  \]
969	  be a resolution of \(A\) (an exact sequence) in which every object \(J^n\) is
970	  right-\(G\)-acyclic in the sense of Definition~\ref{def:right_acyclic}. Then for
971	  every \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) the \(n\)-th right-derived functor of \(G\) at \(A\) is
972	  computed by the resolution: there is an isomorphism in \(\mathcal{B}\)
973	  \[
974	    (R^n G)(A) \;\cong\; H^n\bigl(G(J^\bullet)\bigr),

977	  \(G(J^0) \to G(J^1) \to G(J^2) \to \cdots\) obtained by applying \(G\) degreewise

982	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Say $I^n = 0$ for $n < n_0$. Setting $J^n = \Im(d^n)$ we break

985	% for $n \geq n_0$. These sequences induce distinguished triangles

994	% $$

1001	    lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self, lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective}
1002	    \leanok
1003	  The argument is the classical dimension shift, now assembled from the cosyzygy
1004	  infrastructure and the dimension-shift step proved above. Cut the resolution into
1005	  its cosyzygy short exact sequences \((S_m) : 0 \to Z^m \to J^m \to Z^{m+1} \to 0\)
1006	  with \(Z^0 = A\) and acyclic middle terms, as furnished by
1007	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cosyzygy_ses}.
1008	
1009	  \emph{Degree zero.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution},
1010	  \(H^0(G(J^\bullet)) \cong G(A)\), and by \(R^0 G \cong G\)
1011	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_zero_iso_self}) the right-hand side is \((R^0 G)(A)\).
1012	  This is the case \(n = 0\).
1013	
1014	  \emph{Positive degrees.} Fix \(n \geq 1\). The sequences
1015	  \((S_0), \ldots, (S_{n-2})\) all have acyclic middle terms, so the dimension-shift
1016	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift} applied to them gives, for each \(k \geq 1\),
1017	  isomorphisms \((R^k G)(Z^{m+1}) \cong (R^{k+1} G)(Z^m)\) for \(0 \le m \le n-2\)
1018	  (with \(Z^0 = A\)). Composing them down the staircase
1019	  \[
1020	    (R^n G)(A)
1021	      \;\cong\; (R^{n-1} G)(Z^1)
1022	      \;\cong\; (R^{n-2} G)(Z^2)
1023	      \;\cong\; \cdots
1024	      \;\cong\; (R^{1} G)(Z^{n-1})
1025	  \]
1026	  reduces \((R^n G)(A)\) to a single first-derived functor. (For \(n = 1\) the
1027	  staircase is empty and \((R^1 G)(A) = (R^1 G)(Z^0)\).) Now apply the lowest-degree
1028	  cokernel description (Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}) to \((S_{n-1})\),
1029	  \(0 \to Z^{n-1} \to J^{n-1} \to Z^n \to 0\), whose middle term \(J^{n-1}\) is
1030	  acyclic:
1031	  \[
1032	    (R^1 G)(Z^{n-1}) \;\cong\; \operatorname{coker}\bigl(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)\bigr).
1033	  \]
1034	  Finally Lemma~\ref{lem:cohomology_of_applied_resolution} identifies this cokernel
1035	  with the cohomology of the applied complex,
1036	  \(\operatorname{coker}(G(J^{n-1}) \to G(Z^n)) \cong H^n(G(J^\bullet))\). Composing
1037	  the staircase, the cokernel description, and this identification gives
1038	  \((R^n G)(A) \cong H^n(G(J^\bullet))\) for every \(n \geq 1\); together with the
1039	  degree-zero case this proves the theorem for every \(n \in \mathbb{N}\).
1040	
1041	  Every long exact sequence consumed above is the one produced by the dimension-shift
1042	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_dimension_shift} and its lowest-degree companion
1043	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_one_iso_coker}, and is therefore grounded --- through the
1044	  horseshoe lift and the complex-level homology long exact sequence on which those
1045	  lemmas rest --- on the injective-resolution computation of \(R^n G\). The injective
1046	  objects supplied by ``enough injectives'' are themselves right-\(G\)-acyclic
1047	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:right_derived_vanishes_injective}), which is what makes an injective
1048	  resolution a special case of an acyclic resolution and reconciles the present
1049	  formula with the defining computation \((R^n G)(A) \cong H^n(G(I^\bullet))\) for an
1050	  injective resolution \(I^\bullet\).
1051	\end{proof}

1005	  \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex of \emph{abelian groups} whose term in

1159	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1160	  covering. The \emph{cover structure presheaf} \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} :

1162	  \[

1166	  X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{obj})\) is the structure presheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\)
1167	  regarded as a presheaf of modules over itself. Concretely
1168	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
1169	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise. The degree-\(0\) term
1170	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 = \coprod_i \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_i)\) of the free
1171	  presheaf complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} carries the
1172	  augmentation map \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) onto this image
1173	  presheaf.
1174	\end{definition}

1176	\begin{lemma}
1177	[The free complex resolves \(\mathcal{O}_U\)]
1178	  \label{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}
1179	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso}
1180	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1181	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1182	  % (statement + proof) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
1183	  % L1198--1284).
1184	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} \subset \mathcal{O}_X$
1185	  % be the image presheaf of the map
1186	  % $\bigoplus j_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_i} \to \mathcal{O}_X$.
1187	  % The chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ of presheaves
1188	  % of Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-map-into} above has homology presheaves
1189	  % $$
1190	  % H_i(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet) =
1191	  % \left\{
1192	  % \begin{matrix}
1193	  % 0 & \text{if} & i \not = 0 \\
1194	  % \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} & \text{if} & i = 0
1195	  % \end{matrix}
1196	  % \right.
1197	  % $$"
1198	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1199	  \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}.}
1200	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1201	  open covering. Let \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) be the cover structure presheaf of
1202	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}, the image presheaf of the
1203	  augmentation \(\bigoplus_i (j_i)_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_i} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) (so
1204	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
1205	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise). Then the homology
1206	  presheaves of the free presheaf complex
1207	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1208	  vanish away from degree \(0\) and equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree
1209	  \(0\):
1210	  \[
1211	    H_i\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\bigr) =
1212	    \begin{cases}
1213	      \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} & i = 0, \\
1214	      0 & i \neq 0.
1215	    \end{cases}
1216	  \]
1217	  Equivalently, the augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to
1218	  K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact as a complex of
1219	  presheaves; that is, \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is a (projective) resolution of
1220	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\), so the augmentation
1221	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\) is a
1222	  quasi-isomorphism — \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is quasi-isomorphic to
1223	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\).
1224	\end{lemma}

1226	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the extended complex $K^{ext}_\bullet$ one gets by
1227	% putting
1228	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$ in degree $-1$ with the obvious map
1229	% $K(\mathcal{U})_0 =
1230	% \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}} \to
1231	% \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$.
1232	% It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended complex over
1233	% any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex.
1234	% In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
1235	% $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex.
1236	% Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1237	% if $i \in I_1$.
1238	% ...
1239	% If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1240	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
1241	% ...
1242	% Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1243	% $h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)$
1244	% by the rule
1245	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} = (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
1246	% ... which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
1247	\begin{proof}
1248	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1249	  Homology of a complex in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) is computed
1250	  \emph{objectwise}: kernels, cokernels and hence homology presheaves are formed
1251	  open-by-open, so exactness of a complex of presheaves is checked sectionwise. It
1252	  therefore suffices to show that for every open \(W \subset X\) the extended complex
1253	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-modules
1254	  \[
1255	    \cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1(W) \to K(\mathcal{U})_0(W) \to
1256	      \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) \to 0
1257	  \]
1258	  is acyclic; in fact it is contractible. Split the index set as \(I = I_1 \amalg
1259	  I_2\), where \(i \in I_1\) iff \(W \subset U_i\). If \(I_1 = \varnothing\) the
1260	  extended complex is identically \(0\). If \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) then
1261	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) and, by the explicit
1262	  description of the free presheaves \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)(W) =
1263	  \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise, \(K(\mathcal{U})_p(W) =
1264	  \bigoplus_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) with the alternating-sum
1265	  differential. Fixing an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\), the prescription
1266	  \[
1267	    h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} =
1268	      \begin{cases}
1269	        s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}} & i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}, \\
1270	        0 & i_0 \neq i_{\mathrm{fix}}
1271	      \end{cases}
1272	  \]
1273	  defines a contracting homotopy: a direct computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\),
1274	  so the identity of \(K^{\mathrm{ext}}_\bullet(W)\) is homotopic to zero. Hence the
1275	  extended complex is exact over every \(W\), which is the claimed vanishing of the
1276	  homology presheaves.
1277	
1278	  This sectionwise reduction is legitimate because homology of a complex of presheaves
1279	  of modules is computed sectionwise: the evaluation functor
1280	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}(-)\,V\) at each open \(V\) preserves
1281	  homology (it is exact, so the instance is inferred directly), whence
1282	  \(H_i(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet)(V) = H_i\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\bigr)\). The
1283	  contracting homotopy \(h\) above is the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map — the same
1284	  combinatorial content as the prepend homotopy of the \texttt{CombinatorialCech.*}
1285	  port appearing in the affine vanishing Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
1286	\end{proof}

1288	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
1289	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
1290	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint}
1291	  \mathlibok
1292	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
1293	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Injective.Basic}).}
1294	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
1295	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
1296	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.
1297	\end{lemma}

1299	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion of sheaves of modules]
1300	  \label{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1301	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction}
1302	  \mathlibok
1303	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
1304	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification}).}
1305	  For the identity ring map \(\alpha = \mathbf{1}\) over a scheme \(X\),
1306	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion
1307	  \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
1308	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules:
1309	  \[
1310	    \operatorname{sheafification} \;\dashv\; \iota,
1311	    \qquad
1312	    \iota = \text{the inclusion } X.\mathrm{Modules} \hookrightarrow
1313	    X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}.
1314	  \]
1315	  The left adjoint sheafification is exact, in particular it preserves
1316	  monomorphisms.
1317	\end{lemma}

1343	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{U} : U =
1344	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be any open covering, and let \(\mathcal{I}\) be an
1345	  \emph{injective} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the {\v C}ech cohomology of

1362	% (or its proof) to see the result."
1363	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective presheaf of
1364	% $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
1365	% In this case the functor $\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})$
1366	% is exact on $\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$. By
1367	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-map-into} we have
1368	% $$
1369	% \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{I})
1370	% =
1371	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}).

1374	% quasi-isomorphic to $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}[0]$. Hence by

1378	\begin{proof}
1379	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
1380	  The argument has two independent parts.
1381	
1382	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
1383	  left adjoint to the inclusion
1384	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1385	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
1386	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
1387	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
1388	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
1389	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
1390	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
1391	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
1392	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1393	
1394	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
1395	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
1396	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
1397	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
1398	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
1399	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
1400	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
1401	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
1402	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
1403	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
1404	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
1405	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
1406	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
1407	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
1408	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\). Hence
1409	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
1410	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
1411	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1412	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
1413	\end{proof}

1416	[Surjectivity on sections from cofinal {\v C}ech-$H^1$ vanishing]
1417	  \label{lem:ses_cech_h1}
1418	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ses_cech_h1}
1419	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
1420	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}
1421	  % (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1592--1628).
1422	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
1423	  % Let
1424	  % $$
1425	  % 0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
1426	  % $$
1427	  % be a short exact sequence of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
1428	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an open subset.
1429	  % If there exists a cofinal system of open coverings $\mathcal{U}$
1430	  % of $U$ such that $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$,
1431	  % then the map $\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)$ is
1432	  % surjective."
1433	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1434	  \texttt{lemma-ses-cech-h1}.}
1435	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and let
1436	  \[
1437	    0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H} \to 0
1438	  \]
1439	  be a short exact sequence of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. Let \(U \subset X\) be
1440	  open. If there is a cofinal system of open coverings \(\mathcal{U}\) of \(U\) with
1441	  \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\), then the map on sections
1442	  \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is surjective. Equivalently, the short exact
1443	  sequence is exact on sections over such \(U\).
1444	\end{lemma}
1445	
1446	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Take an element $s \in \mathcal{H}(U)$. Choose an open
1447	% covering
1448	% $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ such that
1449	% (a) $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ and (b)
1450	% $s|_{U_i}$ is the image of a section $s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)$.
1451	% Since we can certainly find $\mathcal{U}$ such that (b) holds
1452	% it follows from the assumptions of the lemma that we can find
1453	% $\mathcal{U}$ such that (a) and (b) both hold.
1454	% Consider the sections
1455	% $$
1456	% s_{i_0i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
1457	% $$
1458	% Since $s_i$ lifts $s$ we see that $s_{i_0i_1} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0i_1})$.
1459	% By the vanishing of $\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$ we can
1460	% find sections $t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$ such that
1461	% $$
1462	% s_{i_0i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0i_1}}.
1463	% $$
1464	% Then clearly the sections $s_i - t_i$ satisfy the sheaf condition
1465	% and glue to a section of $\mathcal{G}$ over $U$ which maps to $s$.
1466	% Hence we win."
1467	\begin{proof}
1468	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
1469	  Let \(s \in \mathcal{H}(U)\). Choose an open covering \(\mathcal{U} : U =
1470	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) fine enough that (a) \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U},
1471	  \mathcal{F}) = 0\) and (b) each restriction \(s|_{U_i}\) lifts to a section
1472	  \(s_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\); both can be arranged simultaneously because the
1473	  coverings with property (a) are cofinal and (b) holds on any sufficiently fine
1474	  cover (surjectivity of \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{H}\) is a local condition).
1475	  On the double overlaps form
1476	  \[
1477	    s_{i_0 i_1} = s_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - s_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}.
1478	  \]
1479	  Because the \(s_i\) all lift the single section \(s\), the difference
1480	  \(s_{i_0 i_1}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\mathcal{H}\), hence lies in the subsheaf
1481	  \(\mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 i_1})\); the family \((s_{i_0 i_1})\) is a {\v C}ech
1482	  \(1\)-cocycle for \(\mathcal{F}\). By \(\check{H}^1(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
1483	  it is a coboundary: there are \(t_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)\) with
1484	  \(s_{i_0 i_1} = t_{i_1}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}} - t_{i_0}|_{U_{i_0 i_1}}\). Then the
1485	  corrected sections \(s_i - t_i \in \mathcal{G}(U_i)\) agree on overlaps, so by the
1486	  sheaf condition they glue to a global section of \(\mathcal{G}\) over \(U\) whose
1487	  image in \(\mathcal{H}\) is \(s\). Hence \(\mathcal{G}(U) \to \mathcal{H}(U)\) is
1488	  surjective.
1489	\end{proof}

1535	  standard open covers of affine opens as the admissible covers. The intersection of

1546	\begin{lemma}

1550	  \uses{def:cech_complex, lem:injective_cech_acyclic, lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}

1559	  % Assume there exists a set of open coverings $\text{Cov}$
1560	  % with the following properties:

1572	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
1573	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.}
1574	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
1575	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
1576	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :
1577	  U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) has \(U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}\) and
1578	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
1579	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
1580	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
1581	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
1582	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then
1583	  \[
1584	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0 \text{ and any }
1585	      U \in \mathcal{B}.
1586	  \]
1587	  This is the basis-comparison vehicle that upgrades the standard-cover {\v C}ech
1588	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of
1589	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}: instantiating \(\mathcal{B}\) as the affine
1590	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly
1591	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. The formal target of this lemma is the
1592	  \emph{vanishing} conclusion \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\) under
1593	  hypotheses (1)--(3), not an explicit isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
1594	  \mathcal{F}) \cong H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of {\v C}ech and sheaf cohomology; only
1595	  the positive-degree vanishing is needed downstream, and it is all the statement
1596	  establishes.
1597	\end{lemma}

1658	  Say that an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module has \emph{vanishing higher {\v C}ech
1659	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)} if condition (3) holds for it. We argue by a
1660	  dimension shift, taking \(\mathcal{F}\) (with vanishing higher {\v C}ech
1661	  cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\)) as the inductive variable and using no input from
1662	  affine sheaf-cohomology vanishing.

1665	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\). By

1668	  particular for every \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\). Put \(\mathcal{Q} =
1669	  \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving the short exact sequence

1676	  \[

1678	    \qquad U \in \mathcal{B}.
1679	  \]

1681	  \(\mathcal{U} \in \mathrm{Cov}\) is a product of values over elements of
1682	  \(\mathcal{B}\), this section-level exactness yields a short exact sequence of
1683	  {\v C}ech complexes
1684	  \[

1686	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) \to
1687	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) \to 0,
1688	  \]

1690	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
1691	  0\) for \(p > 0\), the long exact sequence forces \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
1692	  \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\): the quotient \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has
1693	  vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(\mathrm{Cov}\).

1696	  \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) over a fixed \(U \in \mathcal{B}\). The
1697	  injective \(\mathcal{I}\) has \(H^n(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(n > 0\). The
1698	  section-level surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) = H^0(U, \mathcal{I}) \to H^0(U,

1703	  long exact sequence with \(H^1(U, \mathcal{I}) = H^2(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) forces
1704	  \(H^2(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). Iterating the dimension shift — at each stage
1705	  \(\mathcal{Q}\) plays the role of \(\mathcal{F}\) — gives \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) =
1706	  0\) for all \(p > 0\) and every \(U \in \mathcal{B}\).

1709	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}); its only {\v C}ech-vanishing input is

1712	  \(\text{affine Serre vanishing} \to \text{this lemma}\) is one-directional and the
1713	  graph is acyclic.

1716	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
1717	
1718	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
1719	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are
1720	acyclic for \(f_*\); the abstract homological-algebra
1721	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
1722	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
1723	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.
1724	
1725	\begin{lemma}
1726	[The augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution]
1727	  \label{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}
1728	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechAugmented_exact}
1729	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1730	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1731	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof: exactness of the
1732	  % extended {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L68--82).
1733	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "We are asked to show that the extended complex
1734	  % \begin{equation}
1735	  % \label{equation-extended}
1736	  % 0 \to
1737	  % M \to
1738	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
1739	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
1740	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
1741	  % \ldots
1742	  % \end{equation}
1743	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
1744	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact.
1745	  % It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
1746	  % is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
1747	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}."
1748	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1749	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
1750	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
1751	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
1752	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
1753	  \[
1754	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
1755	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
1756	    \qquad
1757	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1758	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
1759	  \]
1760	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
1761	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
1762	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
1763	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
1764	\end{lemma}
1765	
1766	\begin{proof}
1767	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
1768	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
1769	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
1770	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
1771	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
1772	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
1773	  localisations
1774	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
1775	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
1776	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
1777	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
1778	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
1779	\end{proof}

1781	\begin{lemma}
1782	[Cohomology sheaf is the sheafification of the objectwise homology]
1783	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
1784	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
1785	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
1786	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
1787	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
1788	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
1789	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
1790	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
1791	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
1792	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
1793	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
1794	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
1795	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
1796	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
1797	  \[
1798	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
1799	      \;\cong\;
1800	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
1801	  \]
1802	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
1803	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
1804	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
1805	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
1806	\end{lemma}
1807	
1808	\begin{proof}
1809	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
1810	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
1811	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
1812	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
1813	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
1814	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
1815	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
1816	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
1817	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
1818	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
1819	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
1820	  additive homology-preserving functor.
1821	\end{proof}

1823	\begin{lemma}
1824	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]

1826	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
1827	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}

1829	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
1830	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
1831	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
1832	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
1833	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
1834	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1835	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
1836	  % associated to the presheaves
1837	  % $$
1838	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
1839	  % $$

1842	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
1843	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
1844	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
1845	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
1846	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
1847	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
1848	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
1849	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
1850	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
1851	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
1852	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
1853	  \[
1854	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
1855	      \;\cong\;
1856	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
1857	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
1858	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
1859	  \]
1860	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
1861	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
1862	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
1863	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
1864	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
1865	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
1866	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)

1962	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the

# Blueprint Review Report

## Slug
iter019

## Iteration
019

## Top-level summaries

### Dependency & isolation findings

- `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` / `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` proof block (line 789):
  `\uses{..., AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact}` — a raw Lean declaration
  name used as a blueprint label. `blueprint-doctor` flags it as a broken ref (`kind: uses`,
  `label: AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact`). `leandag` silently matches it
  to a lean-aux node (so `unknown_uses: []` in the build JSON), but it is not a valid blueprint
  label and must not appear in `\uses{}`.
  **Disposition: wire-up error** — `depDiff_exact` is already part of the parent lemma's own
  `\lean{}` list (line 718) and is consumed implicitly through `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`.
  Fix: remove `AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` from the proof `\uses{}`
  of `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`. No new blueprint node is required.
  **Blocks Lane 3** (CechAcyclic.lean prover dispatch).

## Per-chapter

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex — complete + correct, no notes.

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex — complete + correct, no notes.

### blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex
- **complete**: true
- **correct**: true
- **notes**:
  - **MUST-FIX (broken `\uses{}`)** — `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` proof block, line 789: Lean
    name `AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` appears in `\uses{}` as if it
    were a blueprint label; it is not. Remove it from the proof `\uses{}` list. (The dependency
    is already implied via `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, which lists `depDiff_exact` among
    its own `\lean{}` helpers.) This is the sole must-fix finding; it blocks Lane 3.
  - **Informational** — `def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology` (line 1781): block uses
    `\begin{lemma}` environment but carries a `def:` label prefix. Harmless cosmetic
    inconsistency; leanblueprint ignores label prefixes for dispatch purposes.

## Lane-gate verdicts (directive focus)

### Lane 1 — `CechBridge.lean` → `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`
**GATE CLEARS.**
- Statement: isomorphism of cochain complexes
  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) = Č•(𝒰, F)`, natural in `F ∈ PMod`. Clear, well-scoped.
- Proof sketch: free–forgetful adjunction + Yoneda per multi-index; additive degreewise
  isomorphisms interleave with the alternating-sum differentials. Detail is adequate.
- `\uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}` — both valid labels ✓
- `\lean{}` list (10 helper names): names present in `CechBridge.lean` per grep ✓
- Source: `references/stacks-cohomology.tex` exists on disk ✓; verbatim `SOURCE QUOTE` and
  `SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` present ✓; visible `\textit{Source:}` line ✓

### Lane 2 — `FreePresheafComplex.lean` → `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso` + `def:cover_structure_presheaf`
**GATE CLEARS.**

**`def:cover_structure_presheaf`**
- Has `\leanok`; 11 `\lean{}` helper declarations; clear prose identifying `O_𝒰` as the image
  presheaf of the augmentation; `\uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}` valid ✓
- Source: `references/stacks-cohomology.tex` ✓; verbatim quote ✓; visible source line ✓

**`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`**
- Statement: augmented complex `⋯ → K(𝒰)₀ → O_𝒰 → 0` is exact; equivalently,
  `K(𝒰)_•` is a projective resolution of `O_𝒰[0]`. Clear.
- Proof sketch: homology computed sectionwise; for each open `W` split index set as
  `I = I₁ ⊔ I₂`; fix `i_fix ∈ I₁`; prepend-homotopy `h(s)_{i₀…} = (i₀ = i_fix) s_{i₁…}`
  is contracting; `evaluation` functor preserves homology (exact). Adequate for a prover.
- `\uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}` — both valid ✓
- Source: `references/stacks-cohomology.tex` ✓; verbatim quotes ✓; visible source line ✓

### Lane 3 — `CechAcyclic.lean` → `def:qcoh_sections_localized` + `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` + `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`
**GATE BLOCKED** — the broken `\uses{}` in the `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` proof block (see
must-fix above) must be fixed first. All three target declarations are otherwise
well-formulated:

**`def:qcoh_sections_localized`**
- Statement: `F(D(g)) ≅ M_g` as `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g)`; restriction map
  is the canonical localisation map. Well-scoped.
- Residual gap (affine equivalence `F ≅ tilde(ΓF)`, Stacks 01I8) is explicitly documented
  with the available Mathlib hooks (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_presentation`). No overclaiming.
- Source: `references/stacks-schemes.tex` (Tag 01HV items (4)–(5)) ✓; verbatim quote ✓;
  visible source line ✓

**`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`**
- Statement: complex isomorphism `Č•(𝒰, F) ≅ D•` (localised complex) + equivalence
  `IsZero(homology p) ↔ Function.Exact(d^{p-1}, d^p)`. Well-specified.
- `\lean{}` list (22 helper names including all `AwayComparison.*` and `CechLocalized.*`
  helpers) confirms coverage already built in `CechAcyclic.lean`.
- Source: `references/stacks-coherent.tex` ✓; verbatim quote ✓; visible source line ✓

**`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`**
- Statement: standard-cover section Čech complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` over `Spec R` has
  `H^p = 0` for `p > 0`; correctly restricted to absolute section complex
  (no pushforward `f_*`). Strategy-critic design decision confirmed ✓
- Proof sketch: two-paragraph — (1) reduce to `Function.Exact` via `def:qcoh_sections_localized`
  + `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`; (2) close each node via
  `exact_of_isLocalized_span` + `depDiff_exact` contracting homotopy. Adequate.
- `\uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:standard_affine_cover}` (statement block, line 719) — valid ✓
- **Proof `\uses{}` (line 789): BROKEN** — see must-fix above.
- Source: `references/stacks-coherent.tex` ✓; verbatim quotes ✓; visible source line ✓

**Fast-path option for Lane 3**: after a writer removes `AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact` from the proof `\uses{}` and `lake build` stays green, a scoped re-review of this chapter alone suffices to re-open Lane 3 this iter without burning an iter waiting.

## P5a overclaim check (directive-requested)

`lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf` (lines 1823–1901, re-signed iter-019) does **not**
overclaim. Lines 1869–1879 explicitly state:

> "The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology of
> the preimage, `H^k((f_*I^•)(V)) = H^k(f^{-1}(V), G|_{f^{-1}(V)})`, which uses that
> `I^•|_{f^{-1}(V)}` is again injective over `f^{-1}(V)`, **is supplied at point of use**;
> this lemma records the resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form."

The deferred absolute-cohomology bridge is correctly displaced to the downstream consumers
(`lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, `lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic`). This is
acceptable per the directive; not a finding.

`def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology` (engine block, lines 1781–1821) is correctly
marked Archon-original (NOTE comment, no `% SOURCE:` line, no `\textit{Source:}`) ✓.
`\lean{}` hints (`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, etc.) verified present in
`HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean` ✓.

## leandag / blueprint-doctor summary

- `leandag build --json`: `isolated: 0` (no isolated blueprint nodes), `unknown_uses: []`,
  `conflicts: []`. 24 `unmatched_lean` entries — all expected: 9 are `\mathlibok` Mathlib
  declarations; 15 are project targets not yet formalized. No action required on these.
- `archon blueprint-doctor --json`: one `broken_refs` entry (the Lane-3 must-fix above).
  Zero `malformed_refs` (no `undefined-macro`, `math-delim`, `literal-ref`, `bare-label`).
  Zero `orphan_chapters`, `covers_problems`, `axiom_decls`.

## Severity summary

**must-fix-this-iter**:
1. `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` / `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` proof block: broken
   `\uses{}` containing Lean name `AlgebraicGeometry.CombinatorialCech.depDiff_exact`.
   Disposition: **wire-up error** — remove from proof `\uses{}`. Blocks Lane 3 (CechAcyclic).
   Fast path: writer patch → `lake build` green → scoped re-review → Lane 3 unblocked same iter.

**informational**:
1. `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` / `def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology`:
   `\begin{lemma}` environment with `def:` label prefix — cosmetic mismatch, harmless.

**Overall verdict**: Blueprint is substantially complete and correct across all three chapters.
One must-fix broken `\uses{}` in `lem:cech_acyclic_affine` blocks Lane 3 (CechAcyclic);
Lanes 1 and 2 clear the HARD GATE and may be dispatched immediately.
All three lane targets are mathematically well-formulated and adequate for a prover.
No unstarted phases: all four phases have adequate blueprint coverage.
